sections.title: Market Pulse: Tech Rebound Drives Broad Gains

items.headline: Tech-Led Rally Boosts U.S. Equities as Major Indices Close Week Higher
items.summary: U.S. equity markets finished the week on a strong note, with technology stocks driving significant gains across major indices. A rebound in risk assets, bolstered by positive sentiment around semiconductor stocks and the US-Iran peace deal, propelled the S&P 500, NASDAQ, and Dow Jones Industrial Average upwards. The Federal Reserve maintained its interest rates but signaled potential hikes later in the year amid persistent inflation concerns. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Micron Surges on Strong Demand for AI-Related Memory Chips
STMicroelectronics Rises Amid Broader Semiconductor Strength
Nvidia Up as AI Dominance Continues to Drive Sentiment
SpaceX Stock Slips Following IPO Enthusiasm
Canadian Natural Resources Down Amid Softening Oil Outlook
Dell Technologies Declines, Ending a Period of Strong Growth
Technology Sector Leads Weekly Gains; Energy and Financials Lag
items.summary: The technology sector continued its strong performance, driving market gains for the week, notably boosted by growth stocks and advancements in AI. Basic Materials, Energy, and Communication Services also saw positive monthly performance. Conversely, Energy and Financials sectors experienced some headwinds as oil prices softened following the US-Iran peace deal and interest rate expectations shifted. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Signals Potential Hikes Later in 2026
items.summary: The Federal Reserve, under newly appointed Chair Kevin Warsh, maintained its target range for the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75% at its June 17 meeting. However, policymakers signaled the possibility of future rate hikes by year-end if inflation pressures persist, shifting market expectations from previous easing biases. US inflation accelerated to 4.2%. Not financial advice.

items.headline: SpaceX IPO Dominates Headlines, Nvidia Plans Major Bond Issuance
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX made history with an $85.7 billion IPO on June 12, becoming the largest offering ever and valuing the company above $2 trillion. This fueled investor sentiment towards large technology and innovation-linked names. Separately, Nvidia Corporation announced plans to issue at least $20 billion in investment-grade bonds, signaling continued growth and expansion in the AI chip and data center market. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Upcoming Earnings: Micron and FedEx Set to Report Next Week
items.summary: Investors are looking ahead to key earnings reports next week. Micron Technology ($MU) is set to release its Q3 results around June 24, with strong projections driven by demand for AI-related memory chips. FedEx ($FDX) will report its fiscal Q4 earnings around June 23, with analysts keen to assess shipping volumes and cost controls. Not financial advice.
